  [Laggy George, M.B.B.S, MPH](https://www.linkedin.com/in/laggy-george)
  
  ### Laggy George, M.B.B.S, MPH
  
  8mo
  
  [Julie E. Buring liked this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/laggy-george_laggygeorge-harvardalumni-epidemiology-activity-7353615417023569921-JBcY)
  
  One of the remarkable perks of being a Harvard alum is getting access to engaging conversations with iconic scholars, and this week’s Fireside Chat with Dr. [Julie E. Buring](https://www.linkedin.com/in/julie-e-buring-48a9ba15?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) was truly special.
  Dr. Buring is a name that resonates throughout the research community. Her work in epidemiology and clinical trials, especially landmark studies like the Women’s Health Study and the Physicians’ Health Study, continues to inspire generations of public health professionals and clinician-researchers.
  Her insights on the evolution of clinical research, mentorship, and the enduring role of rigorous science in shaping public health policy were incredibly valuable.
  It was also a pleasure to see [Jamie M. Robertson, PhD, CHSE-A, FSSH](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jamie-m-robertson-phd-chse-a-fssh-7b3a2365?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) again, as a moderator - always thoughtful, always engaging.
  Feeling grateful for opportunities like this that fuel my passion for research and connect me with trailblazers in the field. [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Flaggygeorge&trk=public_profile__reactions-text) [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fharvardalumni&trk=public_profile__reactions-text) [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fepidemiology&trk=public_profile__reactions-text) [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fjulieburing&trk=public_profile__reactions-text) [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fclinicalresearch&trk=public_profile__reactions-text) [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fpublichealth&trk=public_profile__reactions-text) [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Flifelonglearning&trk=public_profile__reactions-text)

    + [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fbrittanycharlton_rfkjr-hhs-jaybhattacharya-activity-7313543490062176256-ZSG9&trk=public_profile__reactions_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)
  
  [Julie E. Buring liked this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/brittanycharlton_rfkjr-hhs-jaybhattacharya-activity-7313543490062176256-ZSG9)
  
  [Brittany Charlton](https://www.linkedin.com/in/brittanycharlton)
  
  ### Brittany Charlton
  
  1y
  
  [Julie E. Buring liked this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/brittanycharlton_rfkjr-hhs-jaybhattacharya-activity-7313543490062176256-ZSG9)
  
  🚨 BREAKING: I'm suing [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Frfkjr&trk=public_profile__reactions-text) @ [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhhs&trk=public_profile__reactions-text) & [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fjaybhattacharya&trk=public_profile__reactions-text) @ [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fnih&trk=public_profile__reactions-text) Canceling medical research grants threatens innovations that benefit ALL Americans, no matter your politics 🇺🇸 👇Why am I standing up?👇
  I’m a scientist, not a lawyer, but I appreciate contract law is complex ⚖️ And yet, NIH's contract cancellations set off my alarm bells soon after the Presidential Inauguration 🔔 Two years ago (!) [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fproject2025&trk=public_profile__reactions-text) outlined a plan for political forces to shape scientific research📝 It aimed to consolidate power in the Executive Branch, threatening HHS & NIH independence📜 It attacked fields like mine (i.e., [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Flgbtq&trk=public_profile__reactions-text) health research) as "junk gender science." For us in targeted fields (e.g., vaccine 💉& climate research), we knew securing \*new\* contracts would become more challenging in any new administration that sets new priorities🌍
  Yet, I remained committed to my health equity research, knowing my \*current\* contracts were safe💪 However, just weeks after inauguration, one of my worst professional fears was realized: NIH started terminating contracts, beginning with several dear colleagues leading transgender health research 🏳️‍⚧️ Though my own grants were not initially affected, I felt morally compelled to speak out. So, I...
  🗂️Gathered tons of source documents & called >100 journalists
  🏛️Contacted elected officials (e.g., [Maura Healey](https://www.linkedin.com/in/agmaurahealey?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) & [Andrea Joy Campbell](https://www.linkedin.com/in/andreajoycampbell?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) ) & civil rights organizations (e.g., [Democracy Forward](https://www.linkedin.com/company/democracyfwd?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) )
  And just days after I went on the record, my grants were terminated 🖤 Maybe a coincidence? 🤷‍♀️
  My current NIH research contracts are worth $15.9 million, of which $5.9 million still needs to be spent to finish our research💵 I have essentially no salary now & may need to shutter our newly launched [LGBTQ Health Center of Excellence—Harvard Chan School & Harvard Pilgrim Health Care Institute](https://www.linkedin.com/company/lgbtqhealthexcellence?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) —a career goal of mine that I finally met when we launched <1 year ago💫
  These grant terminations may end my academic career & I have already been forced to make tough decisions like terminating staff (e.g., our Center's Executive Director💔)
  Philanthropy must now play an even larger role in science; >75% of U.S. professors—including ~12,000 of my [Harvard Medical School](https://www.linkedin.com/school/harvard-medical-school/?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) colleagues—lack traditional tenure protections; we must acquire nearly 100% of our own revenue (e.g., grants, clinical care)💰
  My hope: this lawsuit results in a court order (i.e., a preliminary injunction) & halts terminations💼 I believe these contracts, constitutionally grounded, are binding agreements📝 It's been <100 days since inauguration & I'm concerned about signs of growing authoritarianism🗡️ Yet, there is hope! Executive orders can't rewrite laws & I pray courts ensure justice 🙏 Pursuing truth unites us 🔍 & ensures a healthier future for all 🌈
  Finally, 👏 to my legal team ( [ACLU](https://www.linkedin.com/company/aclu?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , [Protect Democracy](https://www.linkedin.com/company/protect-democracy?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , & [Center for Science in the Public Interest](https://www.linkedin.com/company/center-for-science-in-the-public-interest?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) ) & fellow plaintiffs ( [American Public Health Association](https://www.linkedin.com/company/american-public-health-association?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , [International Union, UAW](https://www.linkedin.com/company/uawinternational?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) & [Ibis Reproductive Health](https://www.linkedin.com/company/ibis-reproductive-health?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) )
  
  [public\_profile\_\_reactions](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/brittanycharlton_rfkjr-hhs-jaybhattacharya-activity-7313543490062176256-ZSG9)

  [Tayyaba Hasan](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tayyaba-hasan-3a862537)
  
  ### Tayyaba Hasan
  
  1y
  
  [Julie E. Buring liked this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/tayyaba-hasan-3a862537_cancerresearch-mentorship-activity-7288312565527400449-gWtY)
  
  I'm excited to share the news that I have been awarded the prestigious Presidential Award for Excellence in Science, Mathematics, and Engineering Mentoring (PAESMEM) by The White House Office of Science and Technology Policy in concert with the National Science Foundation. This award recognizes the hard work, dedication, and significant contributions of a mentor to those typically underrepresented in STEM fields. As well as her scientific achievements.
  As one of only 12 individual recipients of this national award for the latest year, I am thrilled that so many of the 300+ mentees I have worked with over the years have become such successful scientists and business leaders who are making strides to mentor others along their paths. - posted by L Maddox [Mass General Brigham](https://www.linkedin.com/company/mass-general-brigham?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , [Mass General Research Institute](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mass-general-research-institute-b9196b159?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , [Harvard Medical School](https://www.linkedin.com/school/harvard-medical-school/?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , [Wellman Center for Photomedicine](https://www.linkedin.com/company/wellmancenter?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , [The White House](https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-white-house?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , [National Science Foundation (NSF)](https://www.linkedin.com/company/national-science-foundation?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fcancerresearch&trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fmentorship&trk=public_profile__reactions-text)

* [David Olawade (MPH, FRSPH, SFHEA) Medway NHS Foundation Trust • 8K followers](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/david-olawade-mph-frsph-sfhea-29206b101?trk=public_profile_relatedPosts_face-pile-cta) [Our latest paper, "Artificial intelligence in Nigerian oncology practice: A qualitative exploration of oncologists' perspectives," sheds light on the potential of AI to revolutionize cancer care in Nigeria. Through in-depth interviews with 15 oncologists from major healthcare institutions, we uncovered key themes that highlight both the benefits and challenges of AI integration in oncology practice. Nigerian oncologists recognize AI's potential to address workforce shortages, improve diagnostic accuracy, and enhance patient care. However, they also expressed concerns about infrastructure deficits, financial constraints, and insufficient technical expertise. To successfully integrate AI in Nigerian oncology practice, we need to address these challenges head-on. A phased implementation approach, focusing on diagnostic support applications, is recommended. Additionally, investing in digital infrastructure and workforce development is crucial. Despite the challenges, Nigerian oncologists are cautiously optimistic about AI's potential to transform cancer care delivery. By working together to address the barriers and harnessing AI's potential, we can improve patient outcomes and create a brighter future for cancer care in Nigeria. Thanks to all co-authors: Ojima Wada (MPH, PhD) Emmanuel O. * [Dr. Rachele Hendricks-Sturrup Duke-Margolis Institute for… • 4K followers](https://www.linkedin.com/in/dr-rachele-hendricks-sturrup-75216a50?trk=public_profile_relatedPosts_face-pile-cta) [I am pleased to share our latest study, entitled "An Assessment of Real-World Evidence and Other Sources Supporting Payer Coverage Decisions for Pharmacogenomic Testing in Psychiatry." In this study, we performed a qualitative and quantitative assessment of publicly available coverage policies from 14 US payers, examining the number and both the type and source of citations across policies and coverage decisions. Overall, we observed, among several distinct observations, that RWE, among several sources, are cited in many forms and to varying degrees among payers providing coverage decisions for PGx testing in psychiatry, with coverage determinations being largely based on how certain payers interpret evidence on the clinical value of testing. Beneficiaries and providers may have questions or hold concerns about how payer entities engage in the selection, listing, or preponderance of scientific evidence. This is particularly true for the practice of psychiatry and those engaged in psychiatric care and precision medicine. Our goals for this work involve addressing these questions and concerns and inspiring further engagement and research on all aspects of RWE and coverage decisions that affect the translation of various biomedical innovations into routine care.
